user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the rear of a car and the rear bumper/trunk area, which is the claimed damaged region. The deformation is visible enough to assess.
claim_status_justification: The photo shows clear damage at the back of the car, especially the rear bumper area, including crumpling and a dented trunk/rear panel. This supports the customer's claim of new rear-end damage.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the claimed car front bumper area with visible scrape/scratch damage, allowing assessment of the claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a visible scrape/scuff with paint loss on the front bumper corner, matching the claim of a front bumper scratch. img_2 is a wider contextual view and does not add detail, but does not contradict the damage.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the claimed car windshield and visible cracking, which is sufficient to assess the claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a cracked front windshield with a stone-impact-like point and spreading fracture lines, matching the claim. img_2 does not show damage clearly but does not contradict the visible crack in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows a car side mirror, which is the claimed damaged part, and the mirror glass condition is visible enough to assess damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the car's side mirror with visible cracking in the mirror glass, matching the claim that the side mirror was damaged and not sitting right.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the claimed car rear bumper closely enough to assess damage, and img_1 shows visible deformation on the bumper area consistent with the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a clear dent/crease on the rear bumper area of the car, which supports the claim of rear bumper damage from being tapped from behind.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image shows a car, but the claimed part (headlight) is not visible clearly enough to inspect for a crack. There is no usable view of the headlight or front light assembly.
claim_status_justification: The image does not show the claimed headlight, so the alleged crack cannot be assessed from this evidence.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the claimed car side door panel area closely enough to assess surface deformation, and that image shows a dent.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly shows a dent/deformation on the car door panel area, matching the claim of a dent along the side of the car on the door panel.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image shows a car, but the claimed hood scratch is not visible clearly enough to assess. The hood is raised and the visible damage is concentrated in the front-end structure, not a discernible scratch across the top panel.
claim_status_justification: The object type matches, but the specific claimed part (hood/top panel) is not visible in a way that allows scratch assessment. The image instead shows a heavily damaged front end with the hood open, so the claimed scratch cannot be confirmed or ruled out from this photo.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows a laptop with the screen visible enough to inspect, and the display glass has obvious cracking consistent with the claim.
claim_status_justification: The photo shows an open laptop with a visibly cracked display glass, matching the reported screen issue.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image shows the laptop and the hinge/lid area closely enough to assess the claimed mechanical issue. Image 1 provides a usable close-up of the hinge region; image 2 confirms the object type but is less detailed.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 shows visible damage/exposure at the hinge seam consistent with a broken hinge area, which supports the claim that the laptop hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. Image 2 is less informative but does not contradict this.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop keyboard area, which is the claimed affected part, and the visible liquid/wetness is sufficient to assess the reported spill-related condition.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the laptop keyboard area with visible liquid residue/wet patches across the keys and adjacent surface, consistent with the claim that water spilled over the keys and left a stain. Sticky keys cannot be directly verified, but the spill/stain aspect is supported.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images do show a laptop and a corner area, but the close-up is too blurry to clearly assess whether there is an actual dent. The wider shot does not reveal corner damage. Usable evidence of the claimed dent is not sufficient.
claim_status_justification: A laptop corner is visible, but the submitted photos do not clearly show a dent or deformation at that corner. The close-up is too blurry to confirm damage, and the wider image does not show any obvious corner issue.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the claimed object type (laptop) and the claimed part (screen) with visible crack damage that can be assessed.
claim_status_justification: The laptop screen is clearly visible and shows spiderweb cracking consistent with the reported shattered screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows a laptop and the trackpad/palm-rest area closely enough to inspect for physical damage. The visible condition can be assessed from the photo.
claim_status_justification: The photo shows the laptop trackpad/palm-rest area with a faint visible scratch/mark near the front edge, which is consistent with the reported physical damage around the trackpad area. The image does not show a functional failure, only a minor surface mark.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows a package corner, and the claimed corner damage is visible enough to assess.
claim_status_justification: The photo shows a visibly crushed/bent package corner with creasing and seam deformation consistent with the claim of corner damage.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the claimed package and the relevant seal/side area with visible torn packaging damage, which is sufficient to assess the torn packaging claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a torn, split seal/side area on the package consistent with the claim that the seal side was torn/opened. Image img_2 shows an intact box and does not negate the damage shown in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows a package exterior and the affected surface is visible enough to assess a wet-looking stain and exterior damage.
claim_status_justification: The photo shows a cardboard package with a prominent dark stain consistent with wetness on the exterior surface, matching the claim that the package looked water damaged.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the opened package interior and contents area, allowing assessment of whether an item is visible inside. The visible interior in img_1 shows only packing paper and no product.
claim_status_justification: The opened box interior is visible in img_1 and appears filled only with crumpled packing paper, with no product/item visible inside. This supports the claim that the contents are missing from the package.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image does not plausibly show the claimed object type: a shipping box/package exterior. It shows a different packaged item/container instead, so it cannot verify whether the outside shipping box was crushed.
claim_status_justification: The only image shows a can-like retail container, not the claimed shipping box. Because no submitted image plausibly depicts the claimed object type, this contradicts the assertion that the photo is evidence of a crushed outside box.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image clearly shows the package exterior and the claimed seal/corner area with visible tearing, which is sufficient to assess the torn-open package claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a torn-open cardboard package corner/seam with split tamper-evident tape, matching the claim that the package arrived opened and the seal area looked torn.
